Disease_Description: Fungal infection is an inflammatory condition caused by fungi multiplying on the skin. It typically leads to redness, intense itching, and scaling.
Medicine_Description: Antifungal medications work by breaking down the cell walls of the fungi, preventing them from growing and spreading further.

Disease_Description: An allergy is an immune system response to a foreign substance (allergen) that is typically harmless, causing symptoms like sneezing, itching, or swelling.
Medicine_Description: Antihistamines block the release of histamine, a chemical produced by your body during an allergic reaction, thereby stopping the symptoms.

Disease_Description: GERD is a digestive disorder where stomach acid frequently flows back into the tube connecting your mouth and stomach (esophagus).
Medicine_Description: Proton pump inhibitors and antacids neutralize stomach acid and reduce acid production, allowing the esophageal lining to heal.

Disease_Description: Chronic cholestasis occurs when the flow of bile from the liver is reduced or blocked, leading to a buildup of bile acids in the bloodstream.
Medicine_Description: Medications like Ursodiol help improve bile flow, protect liver cells, and reduce the toxic levels of bile acids in your body.

Disease_Description: A drug reaction is an adverse, unintended physical response to a medication, often manifesting as a severe rash, fever, or swelling.
Medicine_Description: Corticosteroids and antihistamines suppress the overactive immune response and immediately reduce severe inflammation and allergic symptoms.

Disease_Description: Peptic ulcers are open sores that develop on the inside lining of the stomach, upper small intestine, or esophagus due to acid damage.
Medicine_Description: Antibiotics eradicate H. pylori bacteria if present, while acid reducers decrease stomach acid to create a healing environment for the sore.

Disease_Description: AIDS is a chronic, potentially life-threatening condition caused by the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), which damages the immune system.
Medicine_Description: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) prevents the virus from multiplying, allowing the immune system to recover and preventing opportunistic infections.

Disease_Description: Type 2 Diabetes is a chronic condition that affects the way your body metabolizes sugar (glucose), leading to elevated blood sugar levels.
Medicine_Description: Medications like Metformin improve your body's sensitivity to insulin and decrease the amount of glucose produced by your liver.

Disease_Description: Gastroenteritis is an intestinal infection causing diarrhea, cramps, nausea, vomiting, and fever, often referred to as stomach flu.
Medicine_Description: Treatment focuses on rehydration solutions and anti-nausea medications. Antibiotics are only used if the cause is definitively bacterial.

Disease_Description: Asthma is a condition in which your airways narrow and swell and may produce extra mucus, causing difficulty breathing and wheezing.
Medicine_Description: Inhaled corticosteroids reduce airway inflammation, while quick-relief inhalers relax the airway muscles to quickly restore normal breathing.

Disease_Description: Hypertension (high blood pressure) is a common condition in which the long-term force of the blood against your artery walls is dangerously high.
Medicine_Description: Antihypertensives (like ACE inhibitors or beta-blockers) relax blood vessels and decrease the heart's workload to safely lower blood pressure.

Disease_Description: A migraine is a severe, throbbing headache often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and extreme sensitivity to light and sound.
Medicine_Description: Triptans and pain relievers work by blocking pain pathways in the brain and constricting blood vessels to stop the migraine attack.

Disease_Description: Cervical spondylosis is age-related wear and tear affecting the spinal disks in your neck, often leading to chronic neck pain and stiffness.
Medicine_Description: Muscle relaxants and NSAIDs reduce severe muscle spasms in the neck and significantly decrease joint inflammation and pain.

Disease_Description: Paralysis from a brain hemorrhage involves loss of muscle function in parts of the body due to bleeding in the brain damaging motor pathways.
Medicine_Description: Initial medical focus is on stabilizing blood pressure and reducing brain swelling. Long-term management relies on neuro-rehabilitation.

Disease_Description: Jaundice is a condition where the skin and the whites of the eyes turn yellow due to a high level of bilirubin, indicating liver distress.
Medicine_Description: Treatment treats the underlying cause (like infection). Medications may be given to alleviate severe itching or aid liver function.

Disease_Description: Malaria is a serious and sometimes fatal disease caused by a parasite that commonly infects a certain type of mosquito which feeds on humans.
Medicine_Description: Antimalarial drugs aggressively target and kill the parasites residing in the liver and red blood cells, curing the infection.

Disease_Description: Chickenpox is a highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy, blister-like rash on the skin, accompanied by fever and fatigue.
Medicine_Description: Antiviral medications can reduce the severity if given early. Antihistamines and calamine lotion are used to soothe intense itching.

Disease_Description: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral infection causing a severe flu-like illness, characterized by high fever, severe body aches, and fatigue.
Medicine_Description: There is no specific antiviral; treatment is purely supportive. Acetaminophen is used for pain; NSAIDs like ibuprofen must be avoided due to bleeding risks.

Disease_Description: Typhoid is a bacterial infection that can lead to a high fever, diarrhea, and vomiting. It is typically transmitted through contaminated food or water.
Medicine_Description: Specific antibiotics are the only effective treatment. They quickly kill the Salmonella bacteria causing the massive systemic infection.

Disease_Description: Hepatitis A is a highly contagious liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus. It ranges from a mild illness to a severe sickness lasting months.
Medicine_Description: There is no specific treatment; the body clears the virus on its own. Supportive care focuses on maintaining hydration and nutrition.

Disease_Description: Hepatitis B is a serious liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus that is easily preventable by a vaccine. It can become a chronic condition.
Medicine_Description: Chronic cases require antiviral medications to slow the virus's ability to damage the liver, combined with regular liver monitoring.

Disease_Description: Hepatitis C is a viral infection that causes liver inflammation, sometimes leading to serious liver damage. The virus spreads through contaminated blood.
Medicine_Description: Direct-acting antiviral (DAA) tablets are highly effective and can cure most cases of Hepatitis C within 8 to 12 weeks.

Disease_Description: Hepatitis D is a severe viral liver disease that only occurs in people who are already infected with the Hepatitis B virus.
Medicine_Description: Treatment involves specialized interferons. It is notoriously difficult to treat, making management of the underlying Hepatitis B crucial.

Disease_Description: Hepatitis E is a liver disease caused by the hepatitis E virus, mainly transmitted through drinking water contaminated with fecal matter.
Medicine_Description: It usually resolves on its own without specific medical treatment. Hospitalization is only required for pregnant women or severe cases.

Disease_Description: Alcoholic hepatitis is inflammation of the liver caused by drinking alcohol. It typically occurs after years of heavy drinking.
Medicine_Description: Corticosteroids may be used to rapidly reduce severe liver inflammation. However, total abstinence from alcohol is the only true treatment.

Disease_Description: Tuberculosis is a potentially serious infectious bacterial disease that mainly affects the lungs. The recovery phase requires months of medication.
Medicine_Description: A strict regimen of multiple powerful antibiotics must be taken for 6-9 months to completely eradicate the resilient TB bacteria.

Disease_Description: The common cold is a viral infection of your nose and throat (upper respiratory tract). It's usually harmless, although it might not feel that way.
Medicine_Description: Decongestants relieve nasal stuffiness, while pain relievers manage headaches and fever. There is no cure; medicines only manage symptoms.

Disease_Description: Bronchial pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in the lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus. Recovery takes time and patience.
Medicine_Description: After the initial course of antibiotics clears the infection, expectorants may still be used to help clear lingering mucus from the lungs.

Disease_Description: Hemorrhoids are swollen veins in your lower rectum and anus, similar to varicose veins. They can cause discomfort, itching, and bleeding.
Medicine_Description: Topical creams containing hydrocortisone reduce swelling and pain. Stool softeners are often prescribed to prevent straining during bowel movements.

Disease_Description: Coronary artery disease develops when the major blood vessels that supply your heart become damaged or diseased, usually due to plaque buildup.
Medicine_Description: Statins aggressively lower cholesterol, while blood thinners prevent clots. Beta-blockers reduce the heart's workload by slowing the heart rate.

Disease_Description: Varicose veins are twisted, enlarged veins. Any superficial vein may become varicosed, but the veins most commonly affected are those in your legs.
Medicine_Description: While surgery is an option for severe cases, compression stockings are the primary medical tool used to forcefully assist blood flow back up to the heart.

Disease_Description: Hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid) is a condition in which your thyroid gland doesn't produce enough of certain crucial hormones, slowing metabolism.
Medicine_Description: Synthetic thyroid hormone (Levothyroxine) is taken daily to precisely restore adequate hormone levels, reversing the symptoms of a slowed metabolism.

Disease_Description: Hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid) occurs when your thyroid gland produces too much of the hormone thyroxine, dangerously accelerating your body's metabolism.
Medicine_Description: Anti-thyroid medications gradually reduce the symptoms by preventing the thyroid gland from producing excess amounts of hormones.

Disease_Description: Hypoglycemia is a condition characterized by an abnormally low level of blood sugar (glucose), your body's main energy source.
Medicine_Description: Immediate treatment requires consuming fast-acting carbohydrates. For severe, emergency cases, a glucagon injection rapidly raises blood sugar.

Disease_Description: Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, occurring when the protective cartilage that cushions the ends of your bones wears down over time.
Medicine_Description: NSAIDs and topical analgesics reduce joint pain and inflammation. In severe cases, corticosteroid injections provide temporary, powerful relief.

Disease_Description: Rheumatoid arthritis is a chronic inflammatory disorder where the body's immune system mistakenly attacks its own tissues, primarily the joints.
Medicine_Description: Disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs) dramatically slow the progression of the disease and save the joints and other tissues from permanent damage.

Disease_Description: BPPV is one of the most common causes of vertigo, a sudden sensation that you're spinning or that the inside of your head is spinning.
Medicine_Description: Medications like Meclizine suppress the vestibular system to reduce severe dizziness and nausea, though physical maneuvers are the true cure.

Disease_Description: Acne is a skin condition that occurs when your hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells, leading to whiteheads, blackheads, or pimples.
Medicine_Description: Topical retinoids unclog pores and prevent dead cells from building up. Oral antibiotics reduce inflammation and kill acne-causing bacteria.

Disease_Description: A UTI is an infection in any part of your urinary system (kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra). It causes a strong, persistent urge to urinate and a burning sensation.
Medicine_Description: A targeted course of antibiotics quickly kills the specific bacteria causing the infection. Pain relievers may be given to numb the bladder.

Disease_Description: Psoriasis is a skin disease that causes a rash with itchy, scaly patches, most commonly on the knees, elbows, trunk, and scalp. It is an immune-mediated disease.
Medicine_Description: Topical corticosteroids reduce inflammation and slow massive skin cell buildup. Systemic biologic drugs target specific parts of the immune system.

Disease_Description: Impetigo is a highly contagious skin infection that mainly affects infants and children, appearing as red sores that quickly rupture, ooze, and form a honey-colored crust.
Medicine_Description: Prescription antibiotic ointments or creams applied directly to the sores quickly clear the infection. Oral antibiotics are used for widespread cases.
